ops.title: evaluated cocoindex-code as a tilth/semble alternative for cc-context; decided keep semble, don't add it
ops.body:
Question raised 2026-06-27: should cc-context selectively use `cocoindex-code` (`ccc`) to address
[[cc-context-architecture]] tilth shortcomings?

**Answer: No.** `cocoindex-code` is a *semble-class* semantic-search tool (its whole surface is
init/index/search/grep/status/mcp/daemon) — it has **no** symbol/grok, deps, structural-diff, or
signature-outline capability, so it cannot substitute for tilth at all. The only overlap is the
semantic-search lane cc-context already serves with **semble** (`router.go` OpSearch/OpRelated).

Ran a throwaway head-to-head spike (semble vs cocoindex, 34 intent_search+navigation gold tasks,
local embedders). Result: **relevance is a wash** (MRR 0.50 vs 0.49 on intent_search; semble R@5=1.00
vs cocoindex 0.78 but cocoindex R@1=0.33 vs 0.11). cocoindex emits ~2.3× fewer tokens (partly tunable
in semble via --max-snippet-lines). cocoindex costs much more operationally: **912 MB** pipx install,
a resident daemon, per-repo on-disk index, and **goes stale without a manual reindex** (freshness test:
semble surfaced new code immediately, cocoindex did not). Latency favored cocoindex but that's a
CLI model-reload artifact — semble runs resident as an MCP child in prod.

**Decision: keep semble, don't add cocoindex.** Revisit only if very-large-monorepo semantic-search
latency becomes a real complaint (then opt-in behind the Backend seam). Cheap follow-up needing no new
backend: trim semble's result payload via --max-snippet-lines/--content in internal/backend/semble.go.

Throwaway harness (uncommitted, deletable): `bench/ccxbench/spike_cocoindex.py`; rerun
`cd bench && .venv/bin/python3 -m ccxbench.spike_cocoindex --k 5 --freshness`. Decision memo was
written to the session scratchpad. No edits to internal/.
